NKJV.1: In those days John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea,
NKJV.2: and saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand!”
NKJV.3: For this is he who was spoken of by the prophet Isaiah, saying: “The voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare the way of the LORD; Make His paths straight.’ ”
NKJV.4: Now John himself was clothed in camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
NKJV.5: Then Jerusalem, all Judea, and all the region around the Jordan went out to him
NKJV.6: and were baptized by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.
NKJV.7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, “Brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
NKJV.8: Therefore bear fruits worthy of repentance,
NKJV.9: and do not think to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ For I say to you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
NKJV.10: And even now the ax is laid to the root of the trees. Therefore every tree which does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NKJV.11: I indeed baptize you with water unto repentance, but He who is coming after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
NKJV.12: His winnowing fan is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clean out His threshing floor, and gather His wheat into the barn; but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”
NKJV.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan to be baptized by him.
NKJV.14: And John tried to prevent Him, saying, “I need to be baptized by You, and are You coming to me?”
NKJV.15: But Jesus answered and said to him, “Permit it to be so now, for thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he allowed Him.
NKJV.16: When He had been baptized, Jesus came up immediately from the water; and behold, the heavens were opened to Him, and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting upon Him.
NKJV.17: And suddenly a voice came from heaven, saying, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

ESV.1: In those days John the Baptist came preaching in the wilderness of Judea,
ESV.2: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”
ESV.3: For this is he who was spoken of by the prophet Isaiah when he said, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness: ‘Prepare  the way of the Lord; make his paths straight.’”
ESV.4: Now John wore a garment of camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.
ESV.5: Then Jerusalem and all Judea and all the region about the Jordan were going out to him,
ESV.6: and they were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
ESV.7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
ESV.8: Bear fruit in keeping with repentance.
ESV.9: And do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father,’ for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children for Abraham.
ESV.10: Even now the axe is laid to the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
ESV.11: “I baptize you with water for repentance, but he who is coming after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
ESV.12: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor and gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
ESV.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to John, to be baptized by him.
ESV.14: John would have prevented him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
ESV.15: But Jesus answered him, “Let it be so now, for thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he consented.
ESV.16: And when Jesus was baptized, immediately he went up from the water, and behold, the heavens were opened to him,  and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming to rest on him;
ESV.17: and behold, a voice from heaven said, “This is my beloved Son,  with whom I am well pleased.”

NASB.1: Now in those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea, saying,
NASB.2: 'Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.'
NASB.3: For this is the one referred to by Isaiah the prophet when he said, 'THE VOICE OF ONE CRYING IN THE WILDERNESS, 'MAKE READY THE WAY OF THE LORD, MAKE HIS PATHS STRAIGHT!''
NASB.4: Now John himself had a garment of camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
NASB.5: Then Jerusalem was going out to him, and all Judea and all the district around the Jordan;
NASB.6: and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, as they confessed their sins.
NASB.7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for baptism, he said to them, 'You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
NASB.8: 'Therefore bear fruit in keeping with repentance;
NASB.9: and do not suppose that you can say to yourselves, 'We have Abraham for our father'; for I say to you that from these stones God is able to raise up children to Abraham.
NASB.10: 'The axe is already laid at the root of the trees; therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NASB.11: 'As for me, I baptize you with water for repentance, but He who is coming after me is mightier than I, and I am not fit to remove His sandals; He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
NASB.12: 'His winnowing fork is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clear His threshing floor; and He will gather His wheat into the barn, but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.'
NASB.13: Then Jesus arrived from Galilee at the Jordan [coming] to John, to be baptized by him.
NASB.14: But John tried to prevent Him, saying, 'I have need to be baptized by You, and do You come to me?'
NASB.15: But Jesus answering said to him, 'Permit [it] at this time; for in this way it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness.' Then he permitted Him.
NASB.16: After being baptized, Jesus came up immediately from the water; and behold, the heavens were opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove [and] lighting on Him,
NASB.17: and behold, a voice out of the heavens said, 'This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well-pleased.'

NLT.1: In those days John the Baptist came to the Judean wilderness and began preaching. His message was,
NLT.2: “Repent of your sins and turn to God, for the Kingdom of Heaven is near. ”
NLT.3:
The prophet Isaiah was speaking about John when he said, “He is a voice shouting in the wilderness,
‘Prepare the way for the LORD’s coming!
Clear the road for him!’”
NLT.4: John’s clothes were woven from coarse camel hair, and he wore a leather belt around his waist. For food he ate locusts and wild honey.
NLT.5: People from Jerusalem and from all of Judea and all over the Jordan Valley went out to see and hear John.
NLT.6: And when they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
NLT.7: But when he saw many Pharisees and Sadducees coming to watch him baptize, he denounced them. “You brood of snakes!” he exclaimed. “Who warned you to flee the coming wrath?
NLT.8: Prove by the way you live that you have repented of your sins and turned to God.
NLT.9: Don’t just say to each other, ‘We’re safe, for we are descendants of Abraham.’ That means nothing, for I tell you, God can create children of Abraham from these very stones.
NLT.10: Even now the ax of God’s judgment is poised, ready to sever the roots of the trees. Yes, every tree that does not produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into the fire.
NLT.11: “I baptize with water those who repent of their sins and turn to God. But someone is coming soon who is greater than I am — so much greater that I’m not worthy even to be his slave and carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
NLT.12: He is ready to separate the chaff from the wheat with his winnowing fork. Then he will clean up the threshing area, gathering the wheat into his barn but burning the chaff with never-ending fire.”
NLT.13: Then Jesus went from Galilee to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
NLT.14: But John tried to talk him out of it. “I am the one who needs to be baptized by you,” he said, “so why are you coming to me?”
NLT.15: But Jesus said, “It should be done, for we must carry out all that God requires. ” So John agreed to baptize him.
NLT.16: After his baptism, as Jesus came up out of the water, the heavens were opened and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and settling on him.
NLT.17: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my dearly loved Son, who brings me great joy.”

WEB.1: In those days, John the Baptizer came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea, saying,
WEB.2: “Repent, for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand!”
WEB.3: For this is he who was spoken of by Isaiah the prophet, saying, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, make ready the way of the Lord. Make his paths straight.”
WEB.4: Now John himself wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
WEB.5: Then people from Jerusalem, all of Judea, and all the region around the Jordan went out to him.
WEB.6: They were baptized  by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.
WEB.7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for his baptism,  he said to them, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
WEB.8: Therefore produce fruit worthy of repentance!
WEB.9: Don’t think to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father,’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
WEB.10: “Even now the ax lies at the root of the trees. Therefore every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down, and cast into the fire.
WEB.11: I indeed baptize  you in water for repentance, but he who comes after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.
WEB.12: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor. He will gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn up with unquenchable fire.”
WEB.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan  to John, to be baptized by him.
WEB.14: But John would have hindered him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and you come to me?”
WEB.15: But Jesus, answering, said to him, “Allow it now, for this is the fitting way for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he allowed him.
WEB.16: Jesus, when he was baptized, went up directly from the water: and behold, the heavens were opened to him. He saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, and coming on him.
WEB.17: Behold, a voice out of the heavens said, “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.”

CSB17.1: In those days John the Baptist came, <sup>ⓜ</sup> preaching in the wilderness of Judea <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.2: and saying, “Repent, because the kingdom of heaven has come near!” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.3:
For he is the one spoken of through the prophet Isaiah, who said: A voice of one crying out in the wilderness:
Prepare the way for the Lord;
make his paths straight! [1] ⓑ
CSB17.4: Now John had a camel-hair garment with a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.
CSB17.5: Then people from Jerusalem, all Judea, and all the vicinity of the Jordan were going out to him,
CSB17.6: and they were baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.7: When he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees <sup>ⓓ</sup> coming to his baptism, he said to them, “Brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath? <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.8: Therefore produce fruit <sup>ⓕ</sup> consistent with <sup>[2]</sup> repentance.
CSB17.9: And don’t presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ <sup>ⓖ</sup> For I tell you that God is able to raise up children for Abraham from these stones.
CSB17.10: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.11: “I baptize you with <sup>[3]</sup> water for repentance, <sup>ⓘ</sup> but the one who is coming after me is more powerful than I. I am not worthy to remove <sup>[4]</sup> his sandals. He himself will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.12: His winnowing shovel is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor and gather his wheat into the barn. But the chaff he will burn with fire that never goes out.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan, to be baptized by him. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.14: But John tried to stop him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and yet you come to me?”
CSB17.15: Jesus answered him, “Allow it for now, because this is the way for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then John allowed him to be baptized.
CSB17.16: When Jesus was baptized, he went up immediately from the water. The heavens <sup>ⓜ</sup> suddenly opened for him, <sup>[5]</sup> and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming down on him. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.17: And a voice from heaven said: “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.” <sup>ⓐ</sup>

AMP.1: In those days John the Baptist appeared, preaching in the Wilderness of Judea [along the western side of the Dead Sea] and saying,
AMP.2: " Repent [change your inner self—your old way of thinking, regret past sins, live your life in a way that proves repentance; seek God's purpose for your life], for the kingdom of heaven is at hand."
AMP.3:
This is the one who was mentioned by the prophet Isaiah when he said,
"The voice of one shouting in the wilderness,
'Prepare the road for the Lord,
Make His highways straight (level, direct)!'"
AMP.4: Now this same John had clothing made of camel's hair and a [wide] leather band around his waist; and his food was locusts and wild honey.
AMP.5: At that time Jerusalem was going out to him, and all Judea and all the district around the Jordan;
AMP.6: and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River, as they confessed their sins.
AMP.7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for baptism, he said to them, "You brood of vipers, who warned you to flee from the [divine] wrath and judgment to come?
AMP.8: So produce fruit that is consistent with repentance [demonstrating new behavior that proves a change of heart, and a conscious decision to turn away from sin];
AMP.9: and do not presume to say to yourselves [as a defense], 'We have Abraham for our father [so our inheritance assures us of salvation]'; for I say to you that from these stones God is able to raise up children (descendants) for Abraham.
AMP.10: And already the axe [of God's judgment] is swinging toward the root of the trees; therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
AMP.11: "As for me, I baptize you with water because of [your] repentance [that is, because you are willing to change your inner self—your old way of thinking, regret your sin and live a changed life], but He (the Messiah) who is coming after me is mightier [more powerful, more noble] than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to remove [even as His slave]; He will baptize you [who truly repent] with the Holy Spirit and [you who remain unrepentant] with fire (judgment).
AMP.12: His winnowing fork is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clear out His threshing floor; and He will gather His wheat (believers) into His barn (kingdom), but He will burn up the chaff (the unrepentant) with unquenchable fire."
AMP.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan [River], to be baptized by him.
AMP.14: But John tried to prevent Him [vigorously protesting], saying, "It is I who need to be baptized by You, and do You come to me?"
AMP.15: But Jesus replied to him, "Permit it just now; for this is the fitting way for us to fulfill all righteousness." Then John permitted [it and baptized] Him.
AMP.16: After Jesus was baptized, He came up immediately out of the water; and behold, the heavens were opened, and he (John) saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove and lighting on Him (Jesus),
AMP.17: and behold, a voice from heaven said, "This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well-pleased and delighted!"

MSG.1: While Jesus was living in the Galilean hills, John, called "the Baptizer," was preaching in the desert country of Judea.
MSG.2: His message was simple and austere, like his desert surroundings: "Change your life. God's kingdom is here."
MSG.3: John and his message were authorized by Isaiah's prophecy: Thunder in the desert! Prepare for God's arrival! Make the road smooth and straight!
MSG.4: John dressed in a camel-hair habit tied at the waist by a leather strap. He lived on a diet of locusts and wild field honey.
MSG.5: People poured out of Jerusalem, Judea, and the Jordanian countryside to hear and see him in action.
MSG.6: There at the Jordan River those who came to confess their sins were baptized into a changed life.
MSG.7: When John realized that a lot of Pharisees and Sadducees were showing up for a baptismal experience because it was becoming the popular thing to do, he exploded: "Brood of snakes! What do you think you're doing slithering down here to the river? Do you think a little water on your snakeskins is going to make any difference?
MSG.8: It's your life that must change, not your skin!
MSG.9: And don't think you can pull rank by claiming Abraham as father. Being a descendant of Abraham is neither here nor there. Descendants of Abraham are a dime a dozen.
MSG.10: What counts is your life. Is it green and blossoming? Because if it's deadwood, it goes on the fire.
MSG.11: "I'm baptizing you here in the river, turning your old life in for a kingdom life. The real action comes next: The main character in this drama—compared to him I'm a mere stagehand—will ignite the kingdom life within you, a fire within you, the Holy Spirit within you, changing you from the inside out.
MSG.12: He's going to clean house—make a clean sweep of your lives. He'll place everything true in its proper place before God; everything false he'll put out with the trash to be burned."
MSG.13: Jesus then appeared, arriving at the Jordan River from Galilee. He wanted John to baptize him.
MSG.14: John objected, "I'm the one who needs to be baptized, not you!"
MSG.15: But Jesus insisted. "Do it. God's work, putting things right all these centuries, is coming together right now in this baptism." So John did it.
MSG.16: The moment Jesus came up out of the baptismal waters, the skies opened up and he saw God's Spirit—it looked like a dove—descending and landing on him.
MSG.17: And along with the Spirit, a voice: "This is my Son, chosen and marked by my love, delight of my life."

NIV.1:
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
In those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the Desert of Judea
NIV.2: and saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.”
NIV.3:
This is he who was spoken of through the prophet Isaiah:
“A voice of one calling in the desert,
‘Prepare the way for the Lord,
make straight paths for him.’”
NIV.4: John’s clothes were made of camel’s hair, and he had a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
NIV.5: People went out to him from Jerusalem and all Judea and the whole region of the Jordan.
NIV.6: Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.
NIV.7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to where he was baptizing, he said to them: “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
NIV.8: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance.
NIV.9: And do not think you can say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.
NIV.10: The ax is already at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
NIV.11: “I baptize you with water for repentance. But after me will come one who is more powerful than I, whose sandals I am not fit to carry. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
NIV.12: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor, gathering his wheat into the barn and burning up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”
NIV.13:
The Baptism of Jesus
Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John.
NIV.14: But John tried to deter him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
NIV.15: Jesus replied, “Let it be so now; it is proper for us to do this to fulfill all righteousness.” Then John consented.
NIV.16: As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and lighting on him.
NIV.17: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.”

NRSVCE.1: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness of Judea, proclaiming,
NRSVCE.2: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
NRSVCE.3:
This is the one of whom the prophet Isaiah spoke when he said, “The voice of one crying out in the wilderness:
‘Prepare the way of the Lord,
make his paths straight.’”
NRSVCE.4: Now John wore clothing of camel’s hair with a leather belt around his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.
NRSVCE.5: Then the people of Jerusalem and all Judea were going out to him, and all the region along the Jordan,
NRSVCE.6: and they were baptized by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.
NRSVCE.7: But when he saw many Pharisees and Sadducees coming for baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
NRSVCE.9: Do not presume to say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our ancestor’; for I tell you, God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.
NRSVCE.10: Even now the ax is lying at the root of the trees; every tree therefore that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
NRSVCE.11: “I baptize you with water for repentance, but one who is more powerful than I is coming after me; I am not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
NRSVCE.12: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor and will gather his wheat into the granary; but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
NRSVCE.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan, to be baptized by him.
NRSVCE.14: John would have prevented him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
NRSVCE.15: But Jesus answered him, “Let it be so now; for it is proper for us in this way to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he consented.
NRSVCE.16: And when Jesus had been baptized, just as he came up from the water, suddenly the heavens were opened to him and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him.
NRSVCE.17: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, the Beloved, with whom I am well pleased.”

NET.1: In those days John the Baptist came into the wilderness of Judea proclaiming,
NET.2: “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near.”
NET.3: For he is the one about whom Isaiah the prophet had spoken: “The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the Lord, make his paths straight.’”
NET.4: Now John wore clothing made from camel’s hair with a leather belt around his waist, and his diet consisted of locusts and wild honey.
NET.5: Then people from Jerusalem, as well as all Judea and all the region around the Jordan, were going out to him,
NET.6: and he was baptizing them in the Jordan River as they confessed their sins.
NET.7: But when he saw many Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, “You offspring of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
NET.8: Therefore produce fruit that proves your repentance,
NET.9: and don’t think you can say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ For I tell you that God can raise up children for Abraham from these stones!
NET.10: Even now the ax is laid at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
NET.11: “I baptize you with water, for repentance, but the one coming after me is more powerful than I am — I am not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
NET.12: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clean out his threshing floor and will gather his wheat into the storehouse, but the chaff he will burn up with inextinguishable fire.”
NET.13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John to be baptized by him in the Jordan River.
NET.14: But John tried to prevent him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and yet you come to me?”
NET.15: So Jesus replied to him, “Let it happen now, for it is right for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then John yielded to him.
NET.16: After Jesus was baptized, just as he was coming up out of the water, the heavens opened and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming on him.
NET.17: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my one dear Son; in him I take great delight.”
